title: arabis arenicola
intro: Arabis arenicola, also known as Mountain Sandwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and protection against infections. Arabis arenicola is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and can also be used in salves and creams to enhance its topical applications.
